mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137684.0 as of Oct 2012 reflects post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS momentum fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This threshold-triggered signal predicts negative 20-day forward returns due to rising discount rates and margin compression expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of Oct 2012 exceeds threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns through accelerated GDP growth expectations and corporate profit expansion.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1518.0 and RSAFS at 372034.0 as of Sep 2012
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 5-day forward returns as market participants reprrice growth expectations downward in response to manufacturing contraction signals.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to produce negative 5d returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.8% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 60-day forward returns when unemployment crosses above this threshold, as it indicates weakening demand and corporate earnings headwinds.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% as of Oct 2012, below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: UNRATE > 4.8% fails to produce negative 60d returns in > 56% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows the fundamental demand trajectory. When annualized quarterly growth exceeds 2.5%, this always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns through sustained economic expansion supporting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11549.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2012
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 54% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0% signals monetary accommodation in an inflationary environment. This regime-conditional interaction mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns as markets anticipate future tightening and real rate compression.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of Oct 2012 exceeds trigger lower bound
regime_hint: monetary policy lag in inflationary environment
failure_condition: Trigger condition produces negative 20d returns in < 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining month-over-month simultaneously signals synchronized supply and labor contraction, indicating recessionary pressure. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 60-day forward returns through deteriorating economic fundamentals.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < -50 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show post-crisis recovery as of Oct 2012
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction or early recession
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO and PAYEMS declines fail to produce negative 60d returns in > 57% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window when both conditions trigger.
